attacking_style: Explosively accelerates in final 100-300m of steep uphill finishes (9-25% gradients), preferring late kick from deep wheel position rather than early attacks. On stage races, probes from 3-9km on key climbs to force rivals' responses.
team_tactics: Movistar builds sustained tempo control on final climbs, positioning him 2nd-5th wheel entering decisive gradients. Team sometimes plants him in early breakaways to serve counter-attacks. Occasionally sacrificed for co-leaders despite superior form.
terrain_preference: Dominates steep punchy finales like Mur de Huy (5 wins) and technical uphill sprints with gradients flattening in final 300m. Struggles on sustained high-altitude climbs and technical descents. Superior on short explosive efforts over long grinding ascents.
vulnerability: Age-related decline evident: needs leadout support where previously solo, dropped early on technical/high-speed racing, crashes eliminating GC campaigns. Loses positioning battles when isolated from teammates. ITT weakness exploitable at 1min+/hour versus specialists.
key_patterns: Won 12+ stages via late uphill sprint kick from 5th wheel, never initiating before 200m
Five Fleche Wallonne wins show unmatched repeatability on specific 9-12% punchy gradients
Podium finishes at 39-41 years old demonstrate exceptional longevity but declining finishing speed
Mistimed/premature attacks (3km+ to go) repeatedly caught, requires sub-500m efforts to succeed
Movistar internal leadership conflicts: sprinted against Quintana, sacrificed for Lopez despite superior positioning
Monument consistency: 10-year top-10 streak but increasingly defensive, rarely initiates winning moves
form_trajectory: negative: 2022 retirement year showed clear decline—needed leadout support at Fleche, dropped early in technical racing, crashed out of Vuelta. Still podium-capable on controlled steep finishes but lost independent attacking capacity.
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 7
